prevalence.trend: Increasing by 2-3% annually due to rising diabetes and hypertension

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study (GBD 2019, IHME, extrapolated to 2024)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Based on KDIGO guidelines: eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m² for >3 months or markers of kidney damage (e.g., albuminuria >30 mg/24h)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted KDIGO with local labs; challenges in rural eGFR accuracy
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Conservative management, ACEi/ARB for early stages; dialysis/transplant for ESRD per ISN guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited dialysis access (only ~30% coverage, 1 center per 1 million people); high out-of-pocket costs; rural-urban disparity; water quality issues exacerbating infections
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Government initiatives like Ayushman Bharat for free dialysis; growing nephrology workforce; increasing awareness campaigns
